title: Agedashi Tofu Crispy Dashi

seo.title: Agedashi Tofu Recipe: Crispy Japanese Fried Tofu
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Agedashi Tofu with our easy recipe! Get perfectly crispy fried tofu in a savory dashi broth. Authentic Japanese vegetarian delight.
seo.h1: Agedashi Tofu Recipe: Crispy Japanese Fried Tofu in Dashi
h2Headings: How to Make Agedashi Tofu Step-by-Step
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of crispy Agedashi Tofu with green onions and grated daikon in a savory dashi broth, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this classic Japanese Agedashi Tofu – golden fried tofu soaked in a flavorful dashi broth, a perfect vegetarian appetizer or light meal.

description: Agedashi Tofu is a traditional Japanese dish featuring delicately fried tofu in a flavorful dashi-based broth. This recipe guides you to achieve perfectly crispy tofu exterior with a soft interior, all bathed in a savory, umami-rich sauce. It is a comforting and elegant vegetarian option, perfect as an appetizer or a light meal.

ingredients.notes: Japanese seven spice, optional for serving

instructions.title: Prepare and Press Tofu
instructions.text: Wrap the block of tofu in several layers of paper towels or a clean kitchen cloth. Place a heavy object on top (like a cutting board with cans) and press for at least 30 minutes to remove excess water. This step is crucial for crispy tofu. Once pressed, cut the tofu into 8-12 uniform cubes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetarian Dashi Broth
instructions.text: In a medium saucepan, combine 2 cups of water with the kombu piece and dried shiitake mushrooms (if using). Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat. Do not boil. Remove the kombu just before the water boils. Continue simmering the shiitake for another 10-15 minutes, then remove. Stir in the soy sauce, mirin, and sugar (if using) into the dashi. Keep the broth warm over very low heat.

instructions.title: Coat the Tofu
instructions.text: Place the potato starch or cornstarch in a shallow dish. Lightly dredge each tofu cube in the starch, ensuring it is evenly coated on all sides. Gently shake off any excess starch. This coating creates the signature crispy crust.

instructions.title: Deep Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pot or wok to 350-360°F (175-180°C). Carefully add the coated tofu cubes to the hot oil in batches, being careful not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 4-6 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Use a slotted spoon to transfer the fried tofu to a wire rack lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Assemble and Garnish
instructions.text: Arrange the crispy fried tofu cubes in individual serving bowls. Ladle a generous amount of the warm dashi broth over and around the tofu. The tofu will absorb some of the broth, becoming tender on the inside while retaining a crispy exterior.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Garnish each bowl with a mound of grated daikon radish and a sprinkle of thinly sliced green onions. For a touch of heat, add a pinch of Shichimi Togarashi. Serve immediately while the tofu is still warm and crispy.

tips: Ensure your tofu is well-pressed to remove as much water as possible. This is the secret to a truly crispy exterior that holds up in the broth.
For a deeper umami flavor in your vegetarian dashi, you can soak the kombu and shiitake in water overnight in the refrigerator before gently simmering.
Serve Agedashi Tofu immediately after assembly. The crispy coating will gradually soften as it sits in the hot broth, though it remains delicious.
article: Agedashi Tofu Crispy Dashi is a beloved Japanese Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
